methods.method_name: Statistical Analysis of Rice Pollen Data
methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the effects of Bt rice pollen on various biological metrics such as pollen consumption, pupation, eclosion rate, and 14-day weight. Different statistical tests were applied based on the data characteristics and assumptions.

steps.description: Student's t-tests were conducted to compare Cry1C and Cry2A contents in rice pollen.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Student's t-tests to compare Cry1C and Cry2A contents

steps.description: One-way ANOVAs followed by Tukey HSD tests were used to determine how the nature of rice pollen and pollen dose affected pollen consumption, pupation and eclosion rate, and 14-day weight.
steps.searchable_summary: perform one-way ANOVA and Tukey HSD tests on pollen consumption and other metrics

steps.description: Kruskal-Wallis tests were used for larval development time data, with pair-wise comparisons using Mann-Whitney U-tests if significant differences were detected.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Kruskal-Wallis and Mann-Whitney U-tests on larval development time

steps.description: The Bonferroni correction was applied to correct for 10 pair-wise comparisons leading to an adjusted a = 0.005.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Bonferroni correction for multiple comparisons

steps.description: At each pollen dose, comparisons were made between each Bt pollen treatment and the control using Dunnett tests.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Dunnett tests for Bt pollen treatment comparisons

steps.description: The mean Cry1C concentrations in rice pollen grains before and after gut passage were compared using Student's t test.
steps.searchable_summary: compare mean Cry1C concentrations before and after gut passage using t-test
inputs: Cry1C concentration data before gut passage
Cry1C concentration data after gut passage

steps.description: SPSS 13.0 for Windows was used for all statistical analyses.
